chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No. 1786 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A 15681/16 2. For orders on office objection 3. For orders on M.A 15682/16 4. For Katcha Peshi. 02.11.2016. Mr. Altaf Hussain Chandio, Advocate for petitioners alongwith petitioner No.2. -.-.-. 1. Granted. 2. Deferred for the time being. 3. True translation of the annexures to be filed on or before the next date of hearing. 4. Today, petitioner No.2 is present alongwith his counsel and submits that on next date of hearing petitioner No.1 will appear in Court. This case has a chequered history as explained by the petitioners’ Counsel, that earlier the learned Sukkur Bench of this Court was pleased to dispose of their earlier petition in favour of present petitioners, inter alia, by directing that the petitioner No.1 should not be arrested in connection with any FIR if lodged against him by the parents of petitioner lady. Thereafter a ‘jirga’ was held in which it was decided that petitioner No.2 Ghulam Rasool should give his sister in marriage to his brother-in-law. However, the said decision of 13.08.2014 available at Page-21 of the case file was vehemently opposed by the present petitioner No.2, whereafter private respondents have started lodging false FIRs against him. From the perusal of record, it appears that vide order dated 24.08.2016 available at Page-33 of the case file, learned 2nd Additional Sessions Judge, Shaheed Benazirabad has acquitted the present petitioner No.2. Office is directed to issue notices to the respondents as well as A.A.G for 22.11.2016. Notices to the private respondents be issued through all modes except publication.

Office is directed to issue notices to the respondents as well as A.A.G for 22.11.2016. Notices to the private respondents be issued through all modes except publication. In the meantime, official respondents/police officials are directed to provide protection to the petitioners in accordance with law. It is further directed that private respondents should desist from taking law in their hands by causing harassment of any nature to the petitioners. The concerned SHO P.S Pabjo, district Shaheed Benazirabad is also directed to ensure presence of private respondents No.8 & 9 in Court on next date of hearing. JUDGE Ali Haider
